steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question. The passage traces two loanwords — "cocoa" and "iguana" — from Indigenous languages into English via Spanish. We need the choice that accurately captures the specific role Spanish plays in this process.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It has served as a medium through which Indigenous languages have influenced English.': Both examples show the same pattern: Indigenous language $\Rightarrow$ Spanish $\Rightarrow$ English. Spanish is the intermediary, or medium. This is precisely what the passage demonstrates. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Its contribution to English vocabulary roughly equals the collective contribution by Indigenous languages.': The passage never compares the size of Spanish's contribution to that of Indigenous languages. This comparison is invented. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It has borrowed words from Indigenous languages and contributed words to them.': The passage shows Spanish borrowing from Indigenous languages, not contributing words back to them. The influence is one-directional. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It adopted Nahuatl and Taino words in approximately equal numbers.': Only one Nahuatl word and one Taino word appear in the passage. No numerical comparison is made; this overgeneralizes from two examples. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
